title: The Ngo Dynasty Building the Nation
summary: The first spring of independence: Ngo Quyen ascended the throne and established the capital in Co Loa, laying the foundation for long-term independence.

slides.text: In 939, Ngo Quyen officially ascended the throne, opening the Ngo Dynasty. This was the first time in over 1,000 years that Vietnam had a truly autonomous king.
slides.imagePrompt: Coronation of King NGO QUYEN, ceremony in an ancient courtyard, citizens kneeling in respect, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: He chose Co Loa - the former capital of An Duong Vuong - as his capital as a statement: We are continuing our ancestors' heroic tradition!
slides.imagePrompt: King NGO QUYEN entering the gates of Co Loa citadel, standard with 'NGO' character flying high, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: King Ngo Quyen abolished the colonial officials, established new administrative positions, and enacted regulations to protect the people's peaceful lives.
slides.imagePrompt: NGO QUYEN issuing decrees to protect the poor, setting up a new administration, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Although his reign was short, historical records honor Ngo Quyen as the 'Progenitor of National Restoration' for the Vietnamese nation.
slides.imagePrompt: Statue or painting of NGO QUYEN looking into the distance with pride, historical legacy, sunset, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Which location did Ngo Quyen choose as his capital when he ascended the throne?
What year did Ngo Quyen ascend the throne?
What title is Ngo Quyen praised as in historical records?
